panels.description: Error rates are a measure of unhandled service exceptions per second. Client errors are excluded when possible. Lower is better

matcher.options: /6h Degradation SLO \(5% of monthly error budget\).*/
/1h Outage SLO \(2% of monthly error budget\).*/

targets.legendFormat: 6h Degradation SLO (5% of monthly error budget)
1h Outage SLO (2% of monthly error budget)

panels.title: nginx Service Error Ratio

targets.expr:
sum(
  label_replace(
    vector(0) and on() (gitlab_service_errors:ratio_1h{type="nginx"}),
    "period", "na", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(1) and on () (gitlab_service_errors:ratio_5m{type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "5m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(2) and on () (gitlab_service_errors:ratio_1h{type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "1h", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(4) and on () (gitlab_service_errors:ratio_30m{type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "30m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(8) and on () (gitlab_service_errors:ratio_6h{type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "6h", "", ""
  )
)

panels.description: The operation rate is the sum total of all requests being handle for all components within this service. Note that a single user request can lead to requests to multiple components. Higher is busier.
panels.title: nginx Service RPS - Requests per Second

panels.title: nginx_ingress SLI Error Ratio

targets.expr:
sum(
  label_replace(
    vector(0) and on() (gitlab_component_errors:confidence:ratio_1h{component="nginx_ingress",confidence="98%",type="nginx"}),
    "period", "na", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(1) and on () (gitlab_component_errors:confidence:ratio_5m{component="nginx_ingress",confidence="98%",type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="nginx_ingress",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "5m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(2) and on () (gitlab_component_errors:confidence:ratio_1h{component="nginx_ingress",confidence="98%",type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (14.4 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="nginx_ingress",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "1h", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(4) and on () (gitlab_component_errors:confidence:ratio_30m{component="nginx_ingress",confidence="98%",type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="nginx_ingress",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "30m", "", ""
  )
  or
  label_replace(
    vector(8) and on () (gitlab_component_errors:confidence:ratio_6h{component="nginx_ingress",confidence="98%",type="nginx"} > on(tier, type, __tenant_id__) group_left() (6 * slo:max:events:gitlab_service_errors:ratio{component="nginx_ingress",type="nginx"})),
    "period", "6h", "", ""
  )
)

panels.description: The operation rate is the sum total of all requests being handle for all components within this service. Note that a single user request can lead to requests to multiple components. Higher is busier.
panels.title: nginx_ingress SLI RPS - Requests per Second

panels.content:
### Description

All requests passing through the Nginx ingress controller. Errors are 5xx status codes.



### Observability Tools
panels.title: Details

panels.description:
Kubernetes containers can have a limit configured on how much CPU they can consume in
a burst. If we are at this limit, exceeding the allocated requested resources, we
should consider revisting the container's HPA configuration.

When a container is utilizing CPU resources up-to it's configured limit for
extended periods of time, this could cause it and other running containers to be
throttled.
 Lower is better.

targets.expr:
clamp_min(
  clamp_max(
    max by(container,pod,shard,type) (
  clamp_min(
    clamp_max(
      sum by (container,pod,shard,type) (
        rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total:labeled{container!="", container!="POD", type="nginx"}[5m])
        unless on(pod) (
          kube_pod_labels:labeled{label_gitlab_com_exclude_saturation_point=~"(^|.*[.])kube_container_cpu_limit([.].*|$)", type="nginx"}
        )
      )
      /
      sum by(container,pod,shard,type) (
        container_spec_cpu_quota:labeled{container!="", container!="POD", type="nginx"}
        /
        container_spec_cpu_period:labeled{container!="", container!="POD", type="nginx"}
      )
      ,
      1)
  ,
  0)
)

  ,1)
,0)

panels.title: kube_container_cpu_limit component saturation: Kube Container CPU over-utilization

panels.description:
Records the total anonymous (unevictable) memory utilization for containers for this
service, as a percentage of the memory limit as configured through Kubernetes.

This is computed using the container's resident set size (RSS), as opposed to
kube_container_memory which uses the working set size. For our purposes, RSS is the
better metric as cAdvisor's working set calculation includes pages from the
filesystem cache that can (and will) be evicted before the OOM killer kills the
cgroup.

A container's RSS (anonymous memory usage) is still not precisely what the OOM
killer will use, but it's a better approximation of what the container's workload is
actually using. RSS metrics can, however, be dramatically inflated if a process in
the container uses MADV_FREE (lazy-free) memory. RSS will include the memory that is
available to be reclaimed without a page fault, but not currently in use.

The most common case of OOM kills is for anonymous memory demand to overwhelm the
container's memory limit. On swapless hosts, anonymous memory cannot be evicted from
the page cache, so when a container's memory usage is mostly anonymous pages, the
only remaining option to relieve memory pressure may be the OOM killer.

As container RSS goes over the container memory request and approaches
container memory limit, OOM kills become much more likely. Consequently,
this ratio is a good leading indicator of memory saturation and OOM risk.
 Lower is better.

targets.expr:
clamp_min(
  clamp_max(
    max by(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) (
  clamp_min(
    clamp_max(
      (
        container_memory_rss:labeled{container!="", container!="POD", type="nginx"}
        unless on(pod) (
          kube_pod_labels:labeled{label_gitlab_com_exclude_saturation_point=~"(^|.*[.])kube_container_rss_limit([.].*|$)", type="nginx"}
        )
      )
      / on(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) group_left()
      max by(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) (
        kube_pod_container_resource_limits:labeled{container!="", resource="memory", type="nginx"} > 0
      )
      ,
      1)
  ,
  0)
)

  ,1)
,0)

panels.title: kube_container_rss_limit component saturation: Kube Container Memory Over-Utilization (RSS)

panels.description:
Records the total anonymous (unevictable) memory utilization for containers for this
service, as a percentage of the memory request as configured through Kubernetes.

This is computed using the container's resident set size (RSS), as opposed to
kube_container_memory which uses the working set size. For our purposes, RSS is the
better metric as cAdvisor's working set calculation includes pages from the
filesystem cache that can (and will) be evicted before the OOM killer kills the
cgroup.

A container's RSS (anonymous memory usage) is still not precisely what the OOM
killer will use, but it's a better approximation of what the container's workload is
actually using. RSS metrics can, however, be dramatically inflated if a process in
the container uses MADV_FREE (lazy-free) memory. RSS will include the memory that is
available to be reclaimed without a page fault, but not currently in use.

The most common case of OOM kills is for anonymous memory demand to overwhelm the
container's memory request. On swapless hosts, anonymous memory cannot be evicted from
the page cache, so when a container's memory usage is mostly anonymous pages, the
only remaining option to relieve memory pressure may be the OOM killer.

As container RSS approaches container memory request, OOM kills become much more
likely. Consequently, this ratio is a good leading indicator of memory saturation
and OOM risk.
 Lower is better.

targets.expr:
clamp_min(
  clamp_max(
    quantile by(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) (
  0.99,
  clamp_min(
    clamp_max(
      (
        container_memory_rss:labeled{container!="", container!="POD", type="nginx"}
        unless on(pod) (
          kube_pod_labels:labeled{label_gitlab_com_exclude_saturation_point=~"(^|.*[.])kube_container_rss_request([.].*|$)", type="nginx"}
        )
      )
      / on(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) group_left()
      max by(container,deployment,pod,shard,type) (
        kube_pod_container_resource_requests:labeled{container!="", resource="memory", type="nginx"} > 0
      )
      ,
      1)
  ,
  0)
)

  ,1)
,0)

panels.title: kube_container_rss_request component saturation: Kube Container Memory Utilization (RSS)
